Title: The origin and evolution of the diosgenin biosynthetic pathway in yam

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against pathogens, pests, and herbivores; precursor for steroidal hormonal drugs
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with origin of the diosgenin biosynthetic gene cluster but no direct catalytic validation reported
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Duplicated copy related to DzinCYP90G6 within the Petrosaviidae-specific P450 gene cluster. 2. Inferred to originate from ancestral DzinCYP90B77. 3. Proposed to play a role in the origin of diosgenin biosynthesis, but its direct metabolic function was not validated.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a clustered paralog retained in species that contain the diosgenin-associated P450 cluster.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing and annotation of D. zingiberensis; RNA-seq; BioProject PRJNA541739, accession VCDL00000000
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be useful for evolutionary studies of pathway origin, but not yet a validated engineering target

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ancestral CYP90B gene inferred to have given rise to DzinCYP90G6 and DzinCYP90B71
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Conserved ancestral gene inferred as progenitor of DzinCYP90G6 and DzinCYP90B71. 2. Represents ancestral brassinosteroid-pathway function prior to neofunctionalization. 3. Supports evolutionary reconstruction of diosgenin pathway origin.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported to be conserved in almost all higher plants and function as campesterol C-22 hydroxylase in the brassinosteroid biosynthetic pathway.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Mainly evolutionary significance rather than direct engineering target

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene deletion in yeast; pathway engineering
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion prevented steroid C3-hydroxyl acetylation and supported downstream steroid metabolism toward cholesterol/diosgenin
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deleted from the starting yeast strain. 2. Removal avoided acetylation of steroid C3-hydroxyl groups. 3. Helped enable cholesterol and diosgenin pathway operation in yeast.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A chassis-optimization gene rather than a native diosgenin biosynthetic enzyme.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful genome-editing target for microbial steroid engineering

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene deletion in yeast; pathway engineering; GC-MS cholesterol profiling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion blocked competing ergosterol biosynthesis and improved cholesterol production for diosgenin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deleted in yeast to block native ergosterol synthesis. 2. Reduced sterol competition. 3. Improved precursor availability for cholesterol and downstream diosgenin production.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A precursor-flux engineering target rather than a native diosgenin biosynthetic gene.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful chassis-engineering target for microbial sterol redirection

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in engineered yeast with plant P450s
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous coexpression in yeast; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provided electron transfer support for DzinCYP90G6/DzinCYP94D144-mediated diosgenin biosynthesis in yeast
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Coexpressed with DzinCYP90G6 in engineered yeast. 2. Supported functional P450 activity in the heterologous pathway. 3. Enabled diosgenin formation with DzinCYP90G6 and DzinCYP94D144.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Accessory redox partner, not itself a pathway catalyst for diosgenin skeleton formation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction in engineered yeast fermentation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful accessory gene for heterologous expression of plant P450 modules

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates cholesterol at C-16 and C-22 to generate a diosgenin-pathway intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream, dedicated step toward diosgenin from cholesterol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate dedicated intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against pathogens, pests, and herbivores; precursor for steroidal hormonal drugs in the pharmaceutical industry
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in rhizome than leaves in D. zingiberensis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in rhizome than leaves in D. zingiberensis; active in engineered yeast during galactose induction
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key dedicated pathway enzyme; identified as the best steroid C-16,22-dihydroxylase among tested orthologs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics; phylogenetic analysis; transcriptomics; heterologous expression in engineered yeast; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes diosgenin biosynthesis from cholesterol; supports highest diosgenin production when combined with VcCYP94N1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing and annotation of D. zingiberensis; RNA-seq; BioProject PRJNA541739, accession VCDL00000000
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful core enzyme for microbial diosgenin production and potential metabolic engineering of Dioscorea; best-performing C-16,22-dihydroxylase for pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Pathway genes including diosgenin biosynthetic genes were associated with promoter CpG density differences between D. zingiberensis and D. rotundata, consistent with expression divergence
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction in engineered yeast fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as the D. zingiberensis ortholog of PpCYP90G4 in the diosgenin pathway. 2. Originated by duplication/neofunctionalization from ancestral CYP90B lineage under positive selection. 3. Coexpression in cholesterol-producing yeast enabled conversion toward diosgenin, and it was the best tested steroid C-16,22-dihydroxylase.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of a Petrosaviidae-specific P450 gene cluster with DzinCYP90B71 and DzinCYP94D144; cluster presence correlated with diosgenin detection across monocots.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxylated cholesterol intermediate in diosgenin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates a diosgenin-pathway sterol intermediate at C-26 to complete conversion toward diosgenin.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly required for formation of target product from cholesterol-derived intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against pathogens, pests, and herbivores; precursor for steroidal hormonal drugs in the pharmaceutical industry
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in rhizome than leaves in D. zingiberensis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in rhizome than leaves in D. zingiberensis; functional in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key dedicated pathway enzyme, but inferior to VcCYP94N1 for yeast diosgenin production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics; phylogenetic analysis; transcriptomics; heterologous coexpression in engineered yeast; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables conversion of cholesterol-derived intermediate to diosgenin in engineered yeast when coexpressed with DzinCYP90G6
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing and annotation of D. zingiberensis; RNA-seq; BioProject PRJNA541739, accession VCDL00000000
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A functional C-26 hydroxylase candidate for diosgenin pathway reconstruction, though VcCYP94N1 performed better in microbial production
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Pathway genes including diosgenin biosynthetic genes were associated with promoter CpG density differences between D. zingiberensis and D. rotundata, consistent with expression divergence
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction in engineered yeast fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as the D. zingiberensis ortholog of PpCYP94D108. 2. Located in the Petrosaviidae-specific P450 gene cluster associated with diosgenin occurrence. 3. Coexpression with DzinCYP90G6 in cholesterol-producing yeast generated diosgenin, validating its C-26 hydroxylase role.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Belongs to a Petrosaviidae-specific CYP94X branch that may have arisen by tandem duplication from CYP94Y.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Heterologous cholesterol biosynthetic pathway supporting diosgenin production
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: More reduced cholesterol-pathway sterol toward cholesterol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces the C7 double bond of 7-dehydrocholesterol-pathway intermediate during cholesterol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in engineered yeast for cholesterol production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway engineering in yeast; gene integration; GC-MS cholesterol profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to cholesterol chassis construction that supports diosgenin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful heterologous precursor-supply enzyme for microbial diosgenin production
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose-shift fermentation used in chassis strain operation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Introduced into yeast with DrDHCR24 to build a cholesterol-producing chassis. 2. Supported 16 mg/l cholesterol accumulation in DG-Cho. 3. Enabled downstream diosgenin production after addition of plant P450s.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core to the synthetic biology section but not part of the natural yam pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Heterologous cholesterol biosynthetic pathway supporting diosgenin production
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces the C24 double bond in sterol intermediates to support cholesterol formation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in engineered yeast for cholesterol production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway engineering in yeast; gene integration; GC-MS cholesterol profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to cholesterol chassis construction that supports diosgenin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful heterologous precursor-supply enzyme for microbial diosgenin production; Gal1 control alleviated cholesterol-associated cell damage
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction in engineered yeast fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Introduced into yeast with DrDHCR7 to build a cholesterol-producing chassis. 2. Gal1-regulated expression was used to alleviate cell damage by cholesterol synthesis. 3. Supported 16 mg/l cholesterol accumulation for downstream diosgenin production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core to the synthetic biology section but not part of the natural yam pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxylated cholesterol intermediate in diosgenin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates the diosgenin-pathway sterol intermediate at C-26 to facilitate diosgenin formation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product with partner hydroxylase pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Best tested steroid C-26 hydroxylase for diosgenin production in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative enzyme combination testing in engineered yeast; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: With DzinCYP90G6, increased diosgenin titer to 10 mg/l, the highest reported in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preferred C-26 hydroxylase for microbial diosgenin production modules
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction in engineered yeast fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tested as a heterologous C-26 hydroxylase in yeast. 2. Outperformed DzinCYP94D144 and other candidates in supporting diosgenin production. 3. Combined with DzinCYP90G6 to reach 10 mg/l diosgenin.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not part of the native D. zingiberensis pathway but essential to the best-performing synthetic pathway.
